name: Caesar Salad

name: Garlic Bread

name: Tomato Soup
description: A velvety smooth soup made from ripe tomatoes, onion, and a swirl of cream, perfect for dipping bread into.

name: Mixed Green Salad
description: A light, refreshing medley of baby greens, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, and a zesty vinaigrette.

name: Bruschetta
description: Toasted ciabatta topped with a vibrant mix of diced tomatoes, fresh basil, garlic, and a drizzle of balsamic glaze.

name: Minestrone
description: A hearty Italian vegetable soup brimming with beans, pasta, zucchini, and tomatoes in a savoury broth.

description: Fluffy baked egg bites seasoned with turmeric, cumin, green chili, and cilantro for a protein-packed Indian-spiced snack.

name: Goma-ae (Sesame Spinach)
description: Blanched spinach dressed in a sweet, nutty sesame sauce with a hint of soy and mirin.
pairingReason: nutty sesame greens to balance grilled proteins

name: Hiyayakko (Chilled Tofu)
description: Silky chilled tofu topped with grated ginger, scallions, bonito flakes, and a drizzle of soy sauce.
pairingReason: cool, silky protein with minimal carbs

name: Kinpira Gobo (Braised Burdock)
description: Julienned burdock root and carrot stir-fried in soy sauce, mirin, and sesame oil until tender-crisp.
pairingReason: earthy root crunch to ground noodle dishes

name: Sunomono (Cucumber Vinegar Salad)
description: Paper-thin cucumber slices in a tangy rice vinegar dressing with toasted sesame and wakame seaweed.
pairingReason: refreshing acidity to cleanse a rich palate

name: Nasu Dengaku (Miso-Glazed Eggplant)
description: Tender roasted eggplant halves glazed with a sweet white miso paste, caramelized under a broiler.
pairingReason: sweet-savory miso depth with every bite

name: Tamagoyaki (Rolled Omelette)
description: A layered Japanese omelette gently seasoned with dashi, soy, and mirin, rolled into a golden log.
pairingReason: light, sweet egg protein to round the plate

name: Tsukemono (Japanese Pickles)
description: An assortment of salt-pickled and vinegar-pickled vegetables — daikon, cucumber, eggplant — served as a palate cleanser.
pairingReason: crunchy, tangy pickles between every bite

name: Agedashi Tofu
description: Lightly fried tofu cubes in a warm dashi-soy broth topped with grated daikon, ginger, and bonito flakes.
pairingReason: crispy-soft tofu in savory dashi broth

name: Ohitashi (Dashi-Soaked Greens)
description: Blanched spinach or komatsuna soaked in a light dashi and soy dressing, topped with bonito flakes.
pairingReason: delicate umami greens to pair with rice

name: Soba Noodles (Buckwheat)
description: Nutty buckwheat noodles served chilled with a dashi-soy dipping sauce, scallions, and wasabi.
pairingReason: low-GI buckwheat noodles for a lighter carb

name: Sigeumchi Namul (Sesame Spinach)
description: Blanched spinach seasoned with sesame oil, garlic, soy sauce, and toasted sesame seeds.
pairingReason: classic banchan greens for every Korean meal

name: Kongnamul (Soybean Sprouts)
description: Soybean sprouts blanched and tossed with sesame oil, garlic, and a pinch of gochugaru chili flakes.
pairingReason: crunchy sprouts to refresh between bold bites

name: Hobak Bokkeum (Zucchini Stir Fry)
description: Thinly sliced zucchini quickly stir-fried with garlic, sesame oil, and a pinch of salt until just tender.
pairingReason: quick veggie banchan for balanced Korean meals

name: Gamja Jorim (Braised Potatoes)
description: Bite-sized potatoes braised in a sweet soy sauce glaze with garlic and sesame seeds until glossy and tender.
pairingReason: sweet-savory glazed potatoes for the table

name: Musaengchae (Spicy Radish Salad)
description: Matchstick-cut Korean radish dressed in gochugaru, vinegar, garlic, and a touch of sugar for a crisp, tangy bite.
pairingReason: crunchy, spicy radish to cut through rich stews

name: Miyeok Muchim (Seaweed Side Salad)
description: Rehydrated wakame seaweed tossed in a tangy sesame-vinegar dressing with garlic and chili flakes.
pairingReason: mineral-rich seaweed for oceanic balance

name: Dubu Jorim (Braised Tofu)
description: Pan-fried tofu slices braised in a spicy soy-gochugaru sauce with scallions and sesame seeds.
pairingReason: spicy braised tofu for plant protein

name: Som Tum (Green Papaya Salad)
description: Shredded green papaya pounded with tomatoes, green beans, peanuts, dried shrimp, lime, and bird's eye chili.
pairingReason: spicy-sour crunch as the quintessential Thai side

name: Nam Tok (Waterfall Beef Salad)
description: Grilled sliced beef tossed with toasted rice powder, lime, fish sauce, shallots, mint, and chili flakes.
pairingReason: herbaceous grilled beef for a protein-rich side

name: Pak Boong (Stir-Fried Morning Glory)
description: Water spinach flash-fried in a blazing wok with garlic, soybean paste, and Thai chili for a smoky, spicy green.
pairingReason: fiery wok-charred greens in seconds

name: Gai Yang (Thai Grilled Chicken)
description: Lemongrass-marinated chicken grilled over charcoal until smoky and charred, served with sweet chili dip.
pairingReason: smoky charcoal chicken alongside sticky rice

name: Yum Woon Sen (Glass Noodle Salad)
description: Tangy glass noodle salad with minced pork, shrimp, celery, onion, peanuts, and a fiery lime dressing.
pairingReason: tangy, slippery noodle salad for textural contrast

name: Pla Rad Prik (Crispy Fish with Chili Sauce)
description: Whole deep-fried fish draped in a sweet-sour-spicy tamarind and chili sauce with crispy shallots.
pairingReason: crispy whole fish for an impressive shared side

name: Tod Mun Pla (Thai Fish Cakes)
description: Bouncy, fragrant fish cakes loaded with red curry paste, kaffir lime, and green beans, served with cucumber relish.
pairingReason: herbal, bouncy fish cakes for a Thai starter

name: Mapo Tofu
description: Silken tofu simmered in a fiery sauce of doubanjiang, Sichuan peppercorns, minced pork, and fermented black beans.
pairingReason: numbing-spicy tofu for bold Sichuan pairing

name: Garlic Bok Choy
description: Baby bok choy halves seared in a hot wok with garlic, a splash of soy, and a drizzle of sesame oil.
pairingReason: quick garlic greens for every Chinese meal

name: Smashed Cucumber Salad
description: Cucumbers smashed to absorb a bold dressing of garlic, chili oil, black vinegar, and toasted sesame.
pairingReason: garlicky, spicy crunch to cut through oily dishes

name: Hot and Sour Soup
description: A silky egg-drop soup thickened with tofu, wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and balanced with vinegar and white pepper.
pairingReason: warming sour-spicy broth to open the meal

name: Dan Dan Noodles
description: Thin wheat noodles in a nutty, spicy sesame-chili sauce topped with preserved vegetables and minced pork.
pairingReason: numbing-spicy noodles for a Sichuan feast

name: Xiao Long Bao (Soup Dumplings)
description: Delicate steamed dumplings filled with seasoned pork and a burst of hot, savory broth inside each bite.
pairingReason: soupy dumplings for an elegant dim sum starter

name: Wood Ear Mushroom Salad
description: Sliced wood ear mushrooms tossed in a tangy dressing of black vinegar, chili oil, cilantro, and garlic.
pairingReason: crunchy mushrooms for a refreshing cold dish

name: Steamed Fish with Ginger & Scallion
description: A whole white fish steamed to perfection with julienned ginger, scallions, and a drizzle of hot soy-sesame oil.
pairingReason: clean, delicate steamed fish for a healthy plate

name: Gỏi Cuốn (Fresh Spring Rolls)
description: Translucent rice paper rolls stuffed with shrimp, vermicelli, herbs, lettuce, and served with peanut hoisin dip.
pairingReason: light, fresh rolls to start any Vietnamese meal

name: Rau Muống Xào Tỏi (Garlic Water Spinach)
description: Water spinach wok-tossed with garlic in high heat, finished with fish sauce and a squeeze of lime.
pairingReason: garlicky greens — Vietnam's everyday veggie

name: Canh Chua (Vietnamese Sour Soup)
description: A tangy tamarind-based soup with catfish, tomatoes, pineapple, bean sprouts, and fresh herbs.
pairingReason: sour-sweet soup for a balanced Vietnamese table

name: Bún Chả (Grilled Pork Noodles)
description: Charcoal-grilled pork patties and sliced belly served over rice vermicelli with herbs and sweet fish sauce dip.
pairingReason: smoky grilled pork for a Hanoi classic

name: Gỏi Gà (Vietnamese Chicken Salad)
description: Shredded poached chicken tossed with cabbage, Vietnamese coriander, onion, lime, and fried shallots.
pairingReason: herbaceous chicken salad for fresh contrast

name: Chả Giò (Fried Spring Rolls)
description: Crispy, golden-fried rice paper rolls stuffed with pork, shrimp, mushrooms, and glass noodles, served with nuoc cham.
pairingReason: crispy golden rolls for a crunchy contrast

name: Ensaladang Talong (Eggplant Salad)
description: Char-grilled eggplant peeled and dressed with vinegar, onions, tomatoes, and a drizzle of bagoong.
pairingReason: smoky eggplant to brighten any Filipino meal

name: Atchara (Green Papaya Pickle)
description: Sweet and tangy pickled green papaya with carrots, bell peppers, ginger, and raisins in a vinegar brine.
pairingReason: sweet-sour pickle to cut through rich grilled meats

name: Pinakbet
description: An Ilocano medley of bitter melon, eggplant, squash, okra, and string beans simmered with bagoong shrimp paste.
pairingReason: vegetable stew bursting with Filipino flavors

name: Ginisang Munggo (Mung Bean Stew)
description: Hearty mung bean stew sauteed with garlic, onions, tomatoes, and leafy greens like malunggay or ampalaya leaves.
pairingReason: fiber-rich bean stew for a nutritious pairing

name: Tortang Talong (Eggplant Omelette)
description: Charred eggplant flattened and coated in beaten egg, pan-fried until golden — a simple Filipino breakfast staple.
pairingReason: eggy charred eggplant for protein and fiber

name: Laing (Taro Leaves in Coconut Milk)
description: Dried taro leaves slow-simmered in rich coconut milk with chili, ginger, and shrimp paste until meltingly tender.
pairingReason: creamy, spicy coconut greens for Bicolano flair

name: Lumpiang Sariwa (Fresh Lumpia)
description: Fresh crepe wrappers filled with julienned jicama, carrots, green beans, and tofu, topped with a sweet peanut sauce.
pairingReason: fresh vegetable rolls with a sweet-savory drizzle

name: Brown Rice Bowl
description: Nutty, chewy brown rice served plain as a fiber-rich, lower-GI alternative to white rice for any Asian cuisine.
pairingReason: wholesome grain base that slows blood sugar rise

name: Cauliflower Rice
description: Finely riced cauliflower sauteed with a touch of oil and salt — a near-zero-carb swap for traditional rice.
pairingReason: ultra-low-carb rice alternative for any curry

name: Shirataki Noodles
description: Translucent, gelatinous noodles made from konjac yam with virtually zero calories and carbs, absorbing any sauce.
pairingReason: zero-carb noodle swap for ramen or stir-fries

name: Chaas (Spiced Buttermilk)
description: A refreshing spiced buttermilk drink seasoned with roasted cumin, curry leaves, and fresh cilantro — the quintessential cooling companion to any Indian meal.
pairingReason: cooling probiotic drink to balance spicy mains

name: Bellpepper Curry
description: Vibrant bell peppers simmered in a fragrant onion-tomato masala with cumin, coriander, and a hint of turmeric — a colorful, mild curry.
pairingReason: mild, colorful vegetable curry to add variety

name: Egg Drop Soup with Veggies
description: A light, savory broth with silky ribbons of egg and tender mixed vegetables — a warm, nourishing Filipino staple.
pairingReason: light brothy warmth alongside heartier Filipino mains
